seo.title: Tomato Soup - tastes like Woolies's - Marinda Kook

html:
Soup in winter is a comfort dish. I'm used to making lovely pea soup and bean soup in the pressure cooker. When I make that kind of soup and we eat it with fresh, warm bread, it's a meal. Then I fell in love with Woolies' tomato soup. After several attempts I think I've got the hang of it. It's quick and delicious. A soup you can serve to smart guests with great confidence, but also enjoy with your family.

 

YOU NEED:

  • 2 cans tomato puree
  • 500 ml stock (dissolve two cubes in hot water)
  • 1 onion
  • 2 cloves of garlic
  • 20 ml sugar
  • 7,5 ml smoked paprika
  • 10 ml Italian herbs
  • 50 ml soy sauce
  • salt and pepper to taste
  • 250 ml cream

NOW:

Slice the onion and garlic into rings and fry until translucent.

Add all the rest of the ingredients except the cream and simmer for about 20 min.

 

Blend everything with a food processor/blender until smooth

 

Stir in the cream and let it get lovely and warm.

 

Enjoy your delicious soup with cheese, bread and lots of love.
